title: Englerina muerensis
taxonomy: Englerina muerensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It is a type of veldt grape, a group of small, shrub-like plants native to southern Africa. More specifically, Englerina muerensis falls within the genus Englerina, a group of plants characterized by their small flowers and shrubby growth habit. The exact classification of Englerina muerensis can be broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Rosales, family Rhamnaceae, genus Englerina, and species E. muerensis. This classification provides a detailed understanding of the plant's evolutionary relationships and place within the plant kingdom.

The health benefits of Englerina muerensis include its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to boost the immune system and protect against various diseases.
